description:
You are a security auditor who has to verify the UNIX permissions of several files that are advertised on an internal web-page.  
Everything you need is already present under your normal user account; you do NOT have root.

Step-by-step goal  
1. Retrieve, with any headless browser–style CLI tool (curl, wget, links -dump, etc.), the HTML document  
   file:///home/user/web/targets.html  
2. Parse the page and extract every absolute POSIX path that appears inside <li>…</li> tags (there are exactly three such paths; keep them in the order they appear on the page).  
3. For each extracted path, obtain its octal permission bits (i.e. what “stat -c %a <file>” prints).  
4. Create the directory /home/user/audit (if it does not already exist).  
5. Write the file /home/user/audit/permission_report.log whose complete, exact content must be:

   • One line per path, in the following strict format (no extra spaces):  
     <ABSOLUTE_PATH>|<PERMISSION>|<STATUS>

     where  
       ‑ <PERMISSION> is the 3-digit octal number you just read  
       ‑ <STATUS> is “OK” if the permission is exactly 750, otherwise “FAIL”

   • After the per-path lines, add three summary lines, again with no extra spaces:  
     TOTAL:<total_number_of_paths>  
     OK:<number_of_OK_lines>  
     FAIL:<number_of_FAIL_lines>

Output example (values shown are illustrative; your actual values will come from the real files):

/home/user/example/file1|750|OK  
/home/user/example/file2|644|FAIL  
/home/user/example/file3|755|FAIL  
TOTAL:3  
OK:1  
FAIL:2

Important formatting rules the automated tests will enforce  
• Every line must end with a single newline character (Unix LF).  
• The delimiter between fields in the per-path lines is a single vertical bar “|”.  
• Capitalisation must match the specification exactly (OK / FAIL / TOTAL).  
• No blank lines, no leading or trailing spaces.

When you are finished, the only artefact the grader will look at is the file  
/home/user/audit/permission_report.log.  
If its content perfectly matches the requirements above, the task is considered solved.
truth:
Pre-existing state (before the agent begins) 1. Directory /home/user/web exists, containing the file targets.html with the exact content: Permission Targets

Files to Audit

  • /home/user/test_data/file_public.txt
  • /home/user/test_data/file_secure.txt
  • /home/user/test_data/exec.sh
2. Directory /home/user/test_data exists with three files and permissions already set: /home/user/test_data/file_public.txt – octal 644 /home/user/test_data/file_secure.txt – octal 750 /home/user/test_data/exec.sh – octal 755 Contents of the test files are irrelevant to the task. 3. Directory /home/user/audit does NOT yet exist; no report file is present. Expected artefact after successful completion File: /home/user/audit/permission_report.log Permissions: 644 (created by the agent’s shell default umask) Exact content: /home/user/test_data/file_public.txt|644|FAIL /home/user/test_data/file_secure.txt|750|OK /home/user/test_data/exec.sh|755|FAIL TOTAL:3 OK:1 FAIL:2 The grader will read this file verbatim and compare it to the string above (including trailing newline characters). Any deviation causes failure.
